authors: Marcos Paulo da Silva Rachel R. Mourão Tim P. Vos Marialina C. Antolini Gisele S. Neuls a College of Arts
Federal University of Mato Grosso do Sul
Brazilb Department of Journalism and Media Management
raw_title: News Characteristics, Newsworthiness and Secondary Gatekeeping in Brazil: Influences of Right-Wing Authoritarianism
title_en: News Characteristics, Newsworthiness and Secondary Gatekeeping in Brazil: Influences of Right-Wing Authoritarianism
title_zh: 巴西新闻特征、新闻价值与次级把关：右翼威权主义的影响
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了在巴西，右翼威权主义如何影响新闻的特征、新闻价值判断以及媒体从业者的次级把关行为。它关注政治意识形态如何塑造新闻选择和传播过程。
plain_summary_en: This study examines how right-wing authoritarianism influences news characteristics, judgments of newsworthiness, and secondary gatekeeping by media professionals in Brazil. It focuses on how political ideology shapes news selection and dissemination processes.
research_direction_zh: 探索政治意识形态，特别是右翼威权主义，在数字新闻环境中如何影响新闻生产、把关机制和媒体内容的塑造。
research_direction_en: Investigating how political ideologies, particularly right-wing authoritarianism, influence news production, gatekeeping mechanisms, and media content shaping in digital journalism environments.
